2. All API routes (route.ts files)

4. Component structure within app/
5. How routing groups are organized ((public), (auth), (app))
6. Middleware logic

List every file with a brief description of what it does. Read the key files to understand the architecture.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me compile a comprehensive report:

---

## Thorough Exploration of `/app` Directory - CitedBy.ai

I've completed a comprehensive analysis of the Next.js 14 App Router structure. Here's what I found:

### DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

```
app/
├── layout.tsx                           (Root Layout)
├── globals.css                          (Global styles)
├── (public)/
│   └── page.tsx                         (Landing page - "/" route)
├── (auth)/
│   ├── layout.tsx                       (Auth group wrapper)
│   ├── login/
│   │   └── page.tsx                     (Login page - "/login")
│   └── register/
│       └── page.tsx                     (Register page - "/register")
├── (app)/
│   └── app/
│       ├── layout.tsx                   (Dashboard wrapper with sidebar)
│       ├── page.tsx                     (Dashboard home - "/app")
│       └── projects/
│           ├── page.tsx                 (Projects list - "/app/projects")
│           └── [id]/
│               ├── layout.tsx           (Project detail wrapper with tabs)
│               └── overview/
│                   └── page.tsx         (Project overview - "/app/projects/[id]/overview")
├── api/
│   ├── runs/
│   │   └── start/
│   │       └── route.ts                 (POST: Launch AI analysis scan)
│   ├── stripe/
│   │   ├── checkout/
│   │   │   └── route.ts                 (POST: Create Stripe checkout session)
│   │   ├── portal/
│   │   │   └── route.ts                 (POST: Create Stripe billing portal session)
│   │   └── webhook/
│   │       └── route.ts                 (POST: Stripe webhook handler)
│   └── auth/
└── auth/
    └── signout/
        └── route.ts                     (POST: Logout handler)
```

---

## DETAILED FILE DESCRIPTIONS

### **1. ROOT LAYOUT & GLOBAL STYLES**

#### `app/layout.tsx` (16 lines)
- **Purpose**: Root layout for entire application
- **Key features**:
  - Imports fonts: `DM_Sans` (sans-serif) and `Instrument_Serif` (italic)
  - Sets metadata: title="CitedBy.ai - AEO Optimization Platform"
  - Configures HTML as dark theme (`<html lang="fr" className="dark">`)
  - Applies font variables and global classes
  - Renders all children

#### `app/globals.css` (58 lines)
- **Purpose**: Global Tailwind CSS configuration
- **Key features**:
  - Base Tailwind directives (@tailwind base/components/utilities)
  - CSS variables for light/dark themes
  - Color scheme: primary (white in dark), secondary (dark gray), accent (gray)
  - `.glass-panel` utility class: `bg-white/5 backdrop-blur-md border border-white/5` (glassmorphism effect)

---

### **2. ROUTING GROUPS**

Next.js uses parenthesized directories `(name)` to organize routes without affecting URL structure. CitedBy.ai has 3 routing groups:

#### **(public) GROUP - Public Pages**

**`app/(public)/page.tsx`** (44 lines) - Landing Page (`/`)
- **Purpose**: Marketing landing page accessible to all users
- **Features**:
  - Sticky header with logo and auth links (Login/Register)
  - Hero section with headline "Devenez la Référence des Intelligences Artificielles"
  - Background gradient blur effect
  - Call-to-action buttons linking to `/register`
  - No authentication required

---

#### **(auth) GROUP - Authentication Pages**

**`app/(auth)/layout.tsx`** (9 lines) - Auth Layout
- **Purpose**: Wrapper for login/register pages
- **Features**:
  - `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` - Disables caching for fresh auth state
  - Renders children without additional structure
  - Applies to both `/login` and `/register`

**`app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`** (78 lines) - Login Page (`/login`)
- **Purpose**: User login form
- **Client Component**: `'use client'`
- **Features**:
  - Form with email/password fields
  - Supabase authentication: `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()`
  - Error handling and loading state (Loader2 spinner)
  - Redirects to `/app` on success
  - Link to register page
  - Dark-themed glass panel styling

**`app/(auth)/register/page.tsx`** (79 lines) - Register Page (`/register`)
- **Purpose**: User registration form
- **Client Component**: `'use client'`
- **Features**:
  - Form with email/password fields
  - Supabase authentication: `supabase.auth.signUp()`
  - Error handling and loading state
  - Redirects to `/app` on success (includes comment about email confirmation)
  - Link to login page
  - Similar styling to login page

---

#### **(app) GROUP - Protected Dashboard**

**`app/(app)/app/layout.tsx`** (81 lines) - Dashboard Layout
- **Purpose**: Main authenticated dashboard wrapper
- **Server Component** (uses `async/await` for auth check)
- **Key features**:
  - **Authentication Check**: Fetches user with `supabase.auth.getUser()`, redirects to `/login` if none
  - **Sidebar Navigation**:
    - Logo and branding
    - Navigation links:
      - Tableau de bord (`/app`)
      - Mes Projets (`/app/projects`)
      - Abonnement (`/app/settings/billing`)
      - Paramètres (`/app/settings`)
    - User profile section with email, plan tier, and logout button
  - **Sticky sidebar**: `sticky top-0 h-screen`, hidden on mobile (`hidden md:flex`)
  - **Main content area**: Flexible layout with overflow-y-auto
  - Uses icons from lucide-react

**`app/(app)/app/page.tsx`** (85 lines) - Dashboard Home (`/app`)
- **Purpose**: Main dashboard overview
- **Features**:
  - Header with title "Tableau de Bord"
  - 3-card metrics grid:
    - Score Moyen: 64/100 (+12% vs last month)
    - Citations Détectées: 24 (from 120 prompts tested)
    - Projets Actifs: 1 (with link to projects)
  - 2-column grid layout:
    - Left: Evolution chart placeholder (Recharts)
    - Right: Recent activity feed (2 mock entries)
  - All data is mock/hardcoded for MVP

**`app/(app)/app/projects/page.tsx`** (77 lines) - Projects List (`/app/projects`)
- **Client Component**: `'use client'`
- **Features**:
  - Header with "Mes Projets" and "Nouveau Projet" button
  - Search input for filtering projects
  - Grid layout showing project cards (responsive: 2 cols on md, 3 cols on lg)
  - Mock data: 1 project ("Mon Site Principal", domain: monsite.com)
  - Card shows:
    - Project name and domain (clickable link)
    - AEO Score (65)
    - Link to project dashboard
  - Uses search state: `searchTerm` (not actually filtering in code)

**`app/(app)/app/projects/[id]/layout.tsx`** (56 lines) - Project Detail Wrapper
- **Client Component**: `'use client'`
- **Purpose**: Wrapper for project-specific pages with tab navigation
- **Features**:
  - "Back to projects" link
  - Horizontal tab bar with 5 tabs:
    - Vue d'ensemble (`/app/projects/[id]/overview`)
    - Visibilité IA (`/app/projects/[id]/ai-visibility`)
    - Citations (`/app/projects/[id]/citations`)
    - Recommandations (`/app/projects/[id]/recommendations`)
    - Packs Contenu (`/app/projects/[id]/packs`)
  - Active tab styling: `border-b-2 border-primary` vs `border-transparent`
  - Uses `usePathname()` to detect active tab
  - Uses `cn()` utility for conditional classes
  - **Note**: Only `/overview` is implemented; others are placeholders

**`app/(app)/app/projects/[id]/overview/page.tsx`** (61 lines) - Project Overview (`/app/projects/[id]/overview`)
- **Client Component**: `'use client'`
- **Purpose**: Detailed project dashboard
- **Features**:
  - Header with "Lancer un Scan" button
  - `handleRunAnalysis()` function:
    - POST to `/api/runs/start` with `projectId`
    - Sets loading state
    - Shows alert on completion (TODO: replace with toast)
  - 4-card metrics grid:
    - Score AEO: 65/100
    - Mentions Marque: 12
    - Citations URL: 5
    - Recommandations: 8
  - Large placeholder for 30-day visibility chart
  - All data is mock/hardcoded

---

### **3. API ROUTES**

#### `app/api/runs/start/route.ts` (56 lines) - Launch Analysis
- **Method**: POST
- **Purpose**: Trigger AEO analysis scan for a project
- **Flow**:
  1. Accept `projectId` in request body
  2. Iterate over MOCK_PROMPTS (hardcoded)
  3. For each prompt:
     - Call `generateAiResponse(prompt)` (Gemini API)
     - Score with `calculateAiVisibilityScore()`
     - Push results to array
  4. **TODO**: Save results to Supabase `run_results` table
  5. Return success response with `runId`, `resultsCount`, and sample result
- **Mock Data**:
  - Prompts: Generic AEO questions
  - Project context: brand name, domain, services, competitors
- **Error Handling**: Catches errors, logs, returns 500 (TODO: Use Sentry)

#### `app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts` (64 lines) - Create Checkout Session
- **Method**: POST
- **Purpose**: Initiate Stripe subscription checkout
- **Authentication**: Requires Supabase user
- **Body**: `{ workspaceId, plan }`
- **Flow**:
  1. Authenticate user
  2. Verify user is workspace owner/admin (from `workspace_users` table)
  3. Fetch workspace's existing Stripe customer ID
  4. If none exists, create new Stripe customer and save to DB
  5. Create Stripe checkout session:
     - Customer ID
     - Price ID from PLANS object
     - Mode: `subscription`
     - Success/cancel URLs
     - Metadata: `workspaceId`
  6. Return checkout session URL
- **Error Handling**: Returns 401 (no auth), 403 (forbidden), 400 (missing workspace)

#### `app/api/stripe/portal/route.ts` (40 lines) - Billing Portal Session
- **Method**: POST
- **Purpose**: Redirect user to Stripe customer portal for subscription management
- **Authentication**: Requires Supabase user
- **Body**: `{ workspaceId }`
- **Flow**:
  1. Authenticate user
  2. Verify workspace membership (owner/admin)
  3. Fetch workspace's Stripe customer ID
  4. Create Stripe billing portal session
  5. Return portal URL
- **Error Handling**: Returns 401, 403, 400 (no billing account)

#### `app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts` (90 lines) - Stripe Webhook Handler
- **Method**: POST
- **Purpose**: Handle Stripe webhook events for subscription lifecycle
- **Key Features**:
  - **Admin Client**: Uses `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` to bypass RLS
  - **Signature Verification**: Validates webhook with `stripe.webhooks.constructEvent()`
  - **Idempotence**: Checks if event already processed in `stripe_events_processed` table
  - **Handled Events**:
    1. `checkout.session.completed`: Updates workspace with subscription ID
    2. `customer.subscription.created/updated`: Updates plan, plan_status, current_period_end
    3. `customer.subscription.deleted`: Reverts plan to 'starter', clears subscription ID
  - **Event Recording**: All events logged to `stripe_events_processed`
- **Error Handling**: Returns 400 on signature verification failure

#### `app/auth/signout/route.ts` (10 lines) - Logout Handler
- **Method**: POST
- **Purpose**: Sign out authenticated user
- **Flow**:
  1. Call `supabase.auth.signOut()`
  2. Redirect to `/login` with 302 status
- **Used by**: Form button in dashboard sidebar (`<form action="/auth/signout" method="post">`)

---

## **ROUTING ARCHITECTURE OVERVIEW**

```
URL Structure:

PUBLIC ROUTES (no auth required):
  /                           → (public)/page.tsx
  /login                      → (auth)/login/page.tsx
  /register                   → (auth)/register/page.tsx

PROTECTED ROUTES (requires auth):
  /app                        → (app)/app/page.tsx
  /app/projects               → (app)/app/projects/page.tsx
  /app/projects/[id]/overview → (app)/app/projects/[id]/overview/page.tsx
  /app/projects/[id]/ai-visibility  → (TODO - placeholder in layout)
  /app/projects/[id]/citations      → (TODO - placeholder in layout)
  /app/projects/[id]/recommendations → (TODO - placeholder in layout)
  /app/projects/[id]/packs   → (TODO - placeholder in layout)
  /app/settings              → (TODO)
  /app/settings/billing      → (TODO)

API ROUTES:
  POST /api/runs/start               → Launch analysis
  POST /api/stripe/checkout          → Create checkout session
  POST /api/stripe/portal            → Create billing portal session
  POST /api/stripe/webhook           → Handle Stripe events
  POST /auth/signout                 → Logout
```

## **MIDDLEWARE LOGIC**

**File**: `C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaSV13 - Rick\middleware.ts` (6 lines)

```typescript
import { type NextRequest } from 'next/server'
import { updateSession } from '@/lib/supabase/middleware'

export async function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
  return await updateSession(request)
}

export const config = {
  matcher: [
    '/app/:path*',      // Protected dashboard routes
    '/api/:path*',      // All API routes
    '/login',           // Auth routes
    '/register',
  ],
}
```

**Purpose**: 
- Runs on requests matching the `matcher` patterns
- Calls `updateSession()` from `@/lib/supabase/middleware` to refresh Supabase session
- Used for maintaining authentication state across requests
- Protects `/app/*` routes (redirects to login if not authenticated)

## **NEXT.JS CONFIGURATION**

**File**: `C:\Users\User\Downloads\SaaSV13 - Rick\next.config.mjs`

- **Security Headers**:
  - `X-Frame-Options: DENY` (prevents clickjacking)
  - `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` (prevents MIME sniffing)
  - `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`
  - `Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()` (disables sensitive APIs)
  - `X-DNS-Prefetch-Control: on`
- **Image optimization**: Allows remote images from `lh3.googleusercontent.com` (Google auth avatar CDN)

## **COMPONENT PATTERNS OBSERVED**

1. **Server vs Client Components**:
   - Landing page and layout wrappers are Server Components (async, use Supabase server client)
   - Form pages (login/register) and interactive pages are Client Components (`'use client'`)

2. **Authentication**:
   - Layout components check auth and redirect to `/login` if needed
   - Protected routes wrapped in `(app)` group with auth checks

3. **Styling**:
   - Dark theme via Tailwind CSS variables
   - Glass-morphism cards (`.glass-panel` utility)
   - Lucide icons for UI elements
   - Responsive design (mobile-first, hidden sidebar on mobile)

4. **Data State**:
   - All pages currently use hardcoded mock data
   - Real Supabase integration in place for auth and Stripe
   - API routes prepared for backend logic but most features are MVP stubs

5. **Navigation**:
   - Next.js `Link` component for client-side navigation
   - Sidebar in dashboard layout with active link styling
   - Tab navigation in project detail wrapper
